name: A Shrine to Celeste
description: Every surface honours a woman named Celeste — her portrait above the piano, kept free of dust. Old Neil never stopped mourning her.

name: Vials of His Own Blood
description: Racks of small, carefully dated vials of dark red. Old Neil bled himself in measured amounts over years — unwilling to spill anyone else's.

name: The Alchemical Life Diagram
description: A forbidden 'Alchemical Life' diagram, copied from something that names itself the Hidden Sage — a method to make the dead live again, paid for in blood.

name: Twenty Years of Letters
description: Two decades of letters to Celeste, each recording another failed attempt to bring her back. The hope in them has curdled into something past reason.

name: Antigonus' Notebook
description: A leather notebook whose symbols seem to crawl when unobserved. Plainly a corrupting Beyonder artifact.

name: Spent Revolver
description: A service revolver, one chamber fired, the barrel still faintly warm.

name: The Wrong Reflection
description: The face in the mirror is not the one you remember wearing. You woke in a stranger's life.

name: Nighthawk's Warning
description: A Nighthawk admits the Church is already hunting the notebook — and you with it.

name: The Captain's Charge
description: The Nighthawk captain set you on the trail: Antigonus' notebook is a key the Church will kill for, and the University archives hold his name.

name: Antigonus in the Catalogue
description: The card catalogue confirms Antigonus was real — a long-dead devotee whose key volume has a call number but no book on the shelf.

name: The Missing Volume
description: The restricted shelf has one empty slot, its dust-shadow still fresh. Someone removed Antigonus' volume only recently.

name: The Chain of Owners
description: Abandoned marginalia traces the notebook through Welch and his circle — each reader driven mad, then dead. The list ends at Welch's lodging.

name: The Clerk's Excuse
description: The records clerk insists the restricted volume is 'out for rebinding' — a shade too quickly. He is covering for its disappearance.
